name: Carl Kress
abstract: Carl Kress (October 20, 1907 – June 10, 1965) was an American jazz guitarist.

about_quotes.speaker: Chauncey Morehouse
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: I think you were a friend of Carl Kress. INTERVIEWEE: Yeah, friend.

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: Carl Kress, I played tenor, Carl Kress guitar... And so we had that little band

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: in one rehearsal I have in my book, Chauncey’s in the band. Fidgey McGrath. Fulton McGrath. And Bunny [Berigan]. Dick McDonough and Carl Kress.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli, Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: he taught me “Chicken a la Swing” that Carl Kress/Dick © Fillius Jazz Archive -3- McDonough duet and I played the second part.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli, Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jonah Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I think Carl Kress, if you remember, had something to do with it... Because Carl Kress was a great guitar player, you know.

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: west coast trying to get Stuff Smith back -- but Carl Kress --
